mnemonics: P.A.C. Man: Producers Add (data) to the buffer, Consumers gobble it up and Remove it.
keyPoints: Producers generate data and place it into a shared buffer.
Consumers remove and process data from the shared buffer.
Synchronization (e.g., semaphores) is critical to prevent race conditions and deadlock when accessing the shared buffer.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to synchronize access to the shared buffer, leading to race conditions.
Implementing synchronization incorrectly, causing deadlock where both processes are stuck waiting.
